real_dual_axis_rule.outer_axis: x/z family determined by current inner attitude

candidate_specs.name: tail_y_pair_pos
candidate_specs.rationale: Minimal y-only closure loop from the +Z end state. Probes whether extra inner-axis dwell alone helps dKa_yy / Ka2_y without adding new z-family outer excitation.

candidate_specs.name: tail_y_pair_neg
candidate_specs.rationale: Opposite-sign y-only closure loop. Same theory as tail_y_pair_pos but on the mirrored branch.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_nx_py
candidate_specs.rationale: Open to the -X branch, execute a z-family +Y/-Y excitation-return pair, then close back to +Z. Intended as the safest dKg_zz-targeting motif with net closure.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_nx_ny
candidate_specs.rationale: Mirror of tail_z_return_nx_py on the -Y branch. Tests whether the sign of the late y-face matters for dKg_zz / dKa_yy trade-off.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_px_py
candidate_specs.rationale: Closed z-family excitation-return pair on the +X / +Y branch. Same dKg_zz-oriented motif but mirrored in the x branch.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_px_ny
candidate_specs.rationale: Closed z-family excitation-return pair on the +X / -Y branch.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_sweep_nx
candidate_specs.rationale: Open to -X, keep the z-family outer sweep in the same rotational direction for two nodes, then close. Stronger dKg_zz excitation, but with more risk to already-good channels.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_sweep_px
candidate_specs.rationale: Mirror of tail_z_sweep_nx on the +X branch.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_then_yswap_nx
candidate_specs.rationale: First perform the safe closed z-return motif, then add one extra mirrored y-pair. Intended to preserve the dKg_zz benefit while giving dKa_yy / Ka2_y one more pure-y correction opportunity.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_px_ny_burst10
candidate_specs.rationale: Short append-only burst analogue of the helpful localized z-return idea: same +X/-Y branch as the best dKa_yy-friendly batch-1 return motif, but each appended node is only 10 s total.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_nx_ny_burst10
candidate_specs.rationale: Short burst version of the -X/-Y closed z-return motif, included because it had the cleanest median among the batch-1 return pairs.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_sweep_nx_burst10
candidate_specs.rationale: Short burst version of the batch-1 best family (closed z sweep on the -X branch). Tests whether keeping the same geometry but dramatically shrinking dwell can preserve dKg_zz benefit without wrecking guard channels.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_return_px_ny_burst20
candidate_specs.rationale: Intermediate-duration short z-return tail on the +X/-Y branch, to check whether 10 s nodes are too short but 100 s nodes are too long.

candidate_specs.name: tail_z_sweep_nx_burst20
candidate_specs.rationale: Intermediate-duration version of the best batch-1 geometry.

markov42_rows.rationale: Open to -X, keep the z-family outer sweep in the same rotational direction for two nodes, then close. Stronger dKg_zz excitation, but with more risk to already-good channels.
Mirror of tail_z_return_nx_py on the -Y branch. Tests whether the sign of the late y-face matters for dKg_zz / dKa_yy trade-off.
Closed z-family excitation-return pair on the +X / +Y branch. Same dKg_zz-oriented motif but mirrored in the x branch.
Mirror of tail_z_sweep_nx on the +X branch.

Closed z-family excitation-return pair on the +X / -Y branch.

Short burst version of the batch-1 best family (closed z sweep on the -X branch). Tests whether keeping the same geometry but dramatically shrinking dwell can preserve dKg_zz benefit without wrecking guard channels.
Opposite-sign y-only closure loop. Same theory as tail_y_pair_pos but on the mirrored branch.
Short burst version of the -X/-Y closed z-return motif, included because it had the cleanest median among the batch-1 return pairs.
Short append-only burst analogue of the helpful localized z-return idea: same +X/-Y branch as the best dKa_yy-friendly batch-1 return motif, but each appended node is only 10 s total.
Minimal y-only closure loop from the +Z end state. Probes whether extra inner-axis dwell alone helps dKa_yy / Ka2_y without adding new z-family outer excitation.

best_candidate.name: tail_z_sweep_nx
best_candidate.rationale: Open to -X, keep the z-family outer sweep in the same rotational direction for two nodes, then close. Stronger dKg_zz excitation, but with more risk to already-good channels.

scientific_bottom_line: No appended-tail candidate materially beat the current incumbent. The best append-only result was tail_z_sweep_nx, which shows what tail motif helps most, but the append-only branch still leaves the dominant max channel essentially unresolved.

mechanism_learning.main_blocker: Stronger z sweeps add excitation, but the extra asymmetry starts to leak into protected channels before Ka2_y is repaired enough.
